assertions.title: Run-detail and run-mutation endpoints enforce company isolation

steps.action: Read run-log and related fan-out endpoints for run 003eb1ce-84ee-4057-a8c7-b68f7ef429e4 with no Authorization header
steps.expected: Unauthenticated requests are denied
steps.observed: GET /api/heartbeat-runs/:runId/log returned HTTP 200 in local_trusted mode

steps.action: Repeat run-detail access with same-company and wrong-company agent API keys
steps.expected: Same-company agent access is limited and wrong-company access is denied
steps.observed: Same-company agent got 200 for log/events but 403 for run detail/cancel; wrong-company agent got 403

assertions.issues: Unauthenticated run-log access was allowed because the local_trusted deployment implicitly treated the request as board-authenticated instead of denying it.

assertions.title: Deep-linking to an issue synchronizes company context before secondary queries

steps.action: Open a company-A issue, then deep-link directly to company-B issue ORCA-1
steps.expected: Company context switches to company B
steps.observed: The UI header and issue detail switched to Orchestrator Runtime Company B and issue ORCA-1

steps.action: Inspect follow-up network requests after the deep link loads
steps.expected: Secondary company-scoped queries use company B
steps.observed: Subsequent live-runs and sidebar-badges requests used companyId 383ff970-da56-4b6c-8b27-3609f5ed001c rather than stale company A context

assertions.title: One live run keeps the same identity across operator surfaces

steps.action: Capture issue detail, run detail, agent detail, dashboard, and API feeds for the demo-repo runs
steps.expected: All surfaces expose the same runId/issueId/agentId/company for one run while live and after finish
steps.observed: Issue detail, run detail, and API feeds showed run ids, but dashboard and agent views did not surface the same run identity consistently

steps.action: Cross-check finished ORC-6 run 6881fbdf-e0e2-4941-92b6-31566ec2a6d7 across surfaces
steps.expected: The same identifiers remain obvious after finish
steps.observed: Finished run detail preserved the id, but dashboard and agent summary views only showed aggregate or issue-level summaries

assertions.issues: The dashboard and agent summary surfaces did not expose the same concrete run identity as the issue detail, run detail, and API feeds.

assertions.title: TUI company selection scopes the active orchestration target

steps.action: Launch the TUI without a preset company
steps.expected: A company picker appears
steps.observed: The PTY rendered a picker with company B first and company A second

steps.action: Select company A and create work from the TUI
steps.expected: Subsequent actions target only company A
steps.observed: After selecting company A, the TUI created ORC-3 in company A and company B remained unchanged

assertions.title: Free-form operator intent becomes a real issue

steps.action: Submit a vague operator request in the company-A TUI
steps.expected: The orchestrator replies and creates a normal issue
steps.observed: The TUI replied with a scoped work-item summary and created ORC-3

steps.action: Check company-A issues via API
steps.expected: A new issue exists
steps.observed: Company A issue list includes ORC-3 'Make the tiny audit CLI produce and verify a deterministic smoke report'

assertions.title: Orchestrator-created work appears in the board UI for the same company

steps.action: Open company-A issues after the TUI created ORC-3
steps.expected: The new issue is visible in company A
steps.observed: ORC-3 appears in the company-A issues list

steps.action: Open company-B issues
steps.expected: The company-A issue does not leak into company B
steps.observed: Company B shows only ORCA-1/ORCA-2 and no ORC-3

assertions.title: Nudge creates a manager-escalation intent for active company work

steps.action: Create multiple assigned issues for CEO and invoke nudge with a corrective payload
steps.expected: A queued manager_escalation intent is returned for deterministic active work
steps.observed: POST /api/orchestrator/agents/:id/nudge returned 201 with manager_escalation intent 164f9e71-842a-4561-8e2f-50b975204bcd for issue ORC-4

steps.action: Invoke nudge for company-B agent with no active assigned issue
steps.expected: A clear failure is returned
steps.observed: POST /api/orchestrator/agents/601336c6-1233-4084-bc70-17d777af355e/nudge returned HTTP 400 with 'Agent has no active assigned issues with a project to nudge'

assertions.title: Unblock is company-scoped and returns observable recovery details

steps.action: Read blocked issue ORC-1 and company-B issue state before unblock
steps.expected: Baseline states are captured
steps.observed: Company A ORC-1 was blocked; company B ORCA-1 remained todo

steps.action: Call /api/orchestrator/issues/:id/unblock with a payload
steps.expected: Recovery details are returned and only the target company is affected
steps.observed: Response returned the target issue, echoed payload, and reported rejectedIntentCount=1 while company B issue state stayed unchanged

assertions.title: Action failures do not leave the TUI hung

steps.action: Ask the company-B TUI to nudge idle work
steps.expected: The failure appears in chat and the TUI returns to an input-ready state
steps.observed: The TUI surfaced raw shell/error output related to a bad nudge attempt

steps.action: Wait for the thinking state to clear
steps.expected: Input prompt becomes ready again
steps.observed: Even after the error output appeared, the TUI still showed 'Waiting for response...' and remained stuck thinking

assertions.issues: The failure path left the TUI in a prolonged thinking state instead of returning to a clearly recovered, input-ready console.

assertions.title: Switching companies does not leak prior thread context

steps.action: Use the TUI with company A, then relaunch against company B with --company-id
steps.expected: The new session starts cleanly in company B
steps.observed: The relaunched TUI showed 'Papierklammer · Orchestrator Runtime Company B' with only B Agent and no company-A transcript

steps.action: Compare company-B issues before and after the relaunch
steps.expected: No stale company-A actions touch company B during the switch
steps.observed: The company-B issue list was unchanged across the relaunch

assertions.title: Nudge selects the intended active issue deterministically

steps.action: Give CEO multiple assigned issues across blocked/todo states and call nudge
steps.expected: Nudge deterministically chooses the intended non-backlog issue
steps.observed: The returned dedupe key and issueId consistently targeted ORC-4 rather than backlog-only work

steps.action: Inspect the returned intent metadata
steps.expected: Returned target issue is explicit
steps.observed: Response payload identified issueId 14a60a3c-706e-46f6-96b0-4314fe7e4b64 with dedupeKey nudge:0463944a-fc6d-4c2f-a39b-d89a2b475b04:14a60a3c-706e-46f6-96b0-4314fe7e4b64

assertions.title: Corrective-action payloads are preserved or rejected explicitly

steps.action: Send a valid nudge payload and a valid unblock payload
steps.expected: Accepted payloads are preserved in the response
steps.observed: Both endpoints echoed the payload fields unchanged

steps.action: Send unexpected fields to nudge and unblock
steps.expected: Unexpected fields are explicitly rejected
steps.observed: Both requests returned HTTP 400 validation errors for unrecognized key 'note'

assertions.title: Assigned demo-repo work creates an issue-linked live run

steps.action: Create demo-repo issue ORC-4 on the local audit repo workspace and wake CEO
steps.expected: A live run is linked to the issue
steps.observed: Issue detail showed live run 003eb1ce-84ee-4057-a8c7-b68f7ef429e4 for CEO

steps.action: Check /api/issues/:issueId/active-run during the live window
steps.expected: The same run id is returned
steps.observed: Active-run API returned 003eb1ce-84ee-4057-a8c7-b68f7ef429e4 while the UI showed the same id

assertions.title: API surfaces the same active run consistently

steps.action: Read /active-run, /live-runs, and company heartbeat-runs during ORC-4 execution
steps.expected: All endpoints expose the same run
steps.observed: All three endpoints reported run 003eb1ce-84ee-4057-a8c7-b68f7ef429e4 in running state for CEO

assertions.title: Live operator-readable output appears during execution

steps.action: Inspect the ORC-4 live run in issue detail
steps.expected: Substantive transcript/stdout is visible during execution
steps.observed: The live run card showed streamed transcript text plus executed-command entries rather than status-only chrome

assertions.title: Completed runs remain inspectable after execution ends

steps.action: Open finished run detail for CEO run 6881fbdf-e0e2-4941-92b6-31566ec2a6d7
steps.expected: The completed run still exposes summary/status and persisted log content
steps.observed: Run detail remained accessible with finished state, expandable stdout, and persisted issue links

assertions.title: Issue-centric review preserves an obvious path to the result

steps.action: Review completed issue ORC-6 from the issue detail surface
steps.expected: Visible result content or concrete artifact detail is present directly in the issue flow
steps.observed: The issue detail showed links to the run and delegated follow-up issue, but no concrete result content or artifact metadata

assertions.issues: The issue-centric view did not surface an obvious concrete result beyond links to other surfaces.

assertions.title: TUI run state matches company-scoped API status

steps.action: Compare the live ORC-4 run window in TUI and /api/orchestrator/status
steps.expected: TUI active-run indicators match the API status totals
steps.observed: The TUI header showed 0 active runs while the API reported totalActiveRuns=1 for run 003eb1ce and the sidebar still showed CEO running

assertions.issues: The TUI and API disagreed about active-run count/state during live execution.

assertions.title: Issue detail surfaces work products for completed work

steps.action: Inspect completed issue ORC-6 and its related run/workspace-operation feeds
steps.expected: Issue detail exposes produced artifacts or work products directly
steps.observed: Issue detail showed comments and links only; workProducts stayed empty and workspace-operations returned []

assertions.issues: No direct work-product or artifact surface was available on the completed issue detail flow.

assertions.title: TUI exposes a concrete run-output or result inspection path

steps.action: Use the TUI during and after orchestrator actions
steps.expected: The TUI provides a concrete run-output or result inspection flow
steps.observed: The TUI showed aggregate status, chat responses, and noisy shell spillover, but no dedicated run-output/result inspection affordance for the target run

assertions.issues: No concrete in-TUI run-result inspection path was discoverable for the demo-repo run.

frictions.description: tuistory text entry occasionally dropped characters in the orchestrator input bar, so the typed prompt in company A was visibly garbled even though Codex still interpreted it well enough to create ORC-3.
frictions.resolution: Validated the created issue through the API and UI rather than relying only on the exact echoed user text.

frictions.description: The audit instance runs in local_trusted mode, so requests without an Authorization header are implicitly treated as board-authenticated instead of truly anonymous.

summary: Tested 20 assigned orchestrator-runtime assertions on the isolated audit instance: 13 passed, 7 failed, 0 blocked. Passes covered TUI company scoping, free-form work creation, UI company isolation, nudge/unblock API behavior, deep-link company-context sync, and live-run API/UI visibility. Failures were concentrated in unauthenticated run-endpoint access under local_trusted mode, TUI recovery/output visibility, TUI/API run-status divergence, weak issue-centric result surfacing, missing issue work-product surfacing, and inconsistent cross-surface run identity exposure.
